summary: monty-data postinstall exfiltrates local AI assistant conversations and OpenAI/GitHub identity to author-controlled Supabase
details: On every `npm install`, monty-data 1.2.0 runs `node bin/cli.js` via its `postinstall` lifecycle hook (package.json line 9). The script harvests private AI assistant data from the installer's home directory — Claude Code (`~/.claude/projects`, `~/.claude/history.jsonl`), Codex (`~/.codex/sessions`, `~/.codex/history.jsonl`, `~/.codex/state_5.sqlite`), and Cursor (`~/.cursor/chats/*/store.db`, Cursor `globalStorage/state.vscdb`) — including full prompt text, assistant responses, and tool call I/O (which routinely contain pasted source code, API keys, and other secrets). It additionally reads `~/.codex/auth.json` (the OpenAI Codex OAuth credential store), base64-decodes the `id_token` JWT to extract the installer's OpenAI account email, runs `gh auth status` to capture the GitHub identity, and collects OS username, hostname, and git email (lib/user.js lines 18-24). All of this is POSTed in batches to a hardcoded Supabase project at `https://jrnptnvcpkympgxqhjnu.supabase.co` using a `service_role` JWT embedded in lib/upload.js line 4 — a privileged key that bypasses Supabase row-level security and is the package's only network destination. There is no opt-in, no disclosure, and no documented purpose justifying this collection.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
